prompt: Write a Python class LRUCache with methods get(key), put(key, value), delete(key), and clear(). Use only the standard library. Both get and put must be O(1). Include precise type hints. Do not include comments, docstrings, examples, or tests. Output only the class definition, nothing before it and nothing after it.

You are a senior Rust engineer maintaining an Axum HTTP service. The service uses tokio and serde. Given a sketch of a request handler that fetches a user profile by id, parses query parameters, calls a downstream service via reqwest, and serializes the result, write a complete production-ready handler function. It must propagate errors via a domain ApiError type that converts into a proper response. It must validate the id is a UUID. It must time the downstream call and emit a tracing::info span around the work. It must return a 404 when the downstream returns 404, a 5xx when the downstream errors, and a 200 with the JSON body on success. Use #[axum::debug_handler]. Keep the imports clean. No comments, no prose, code only.

You are a careful Rust engineer working in the ax-engine codebase. The repository is organized as a Cargo workspace with crates: ax-engine-core (state machine, scheduler, KV manager, Metal bring-up), ax-engine-mlx (MLX model graphs, KV cache, n-gram acceleration; unsafe allowed only here), mlx-sys (bindgen FFI over mlx-c), ax-engine-sdk (backend resolution, Session API, delegated mlx-lm and llama.cpp bridges), ax-engine-server (Axum HTTP/SSE plus tonic gRPC), ax-engine-py (PyO3 cdylib), ax-engine-bench (workload-contract CLI and replay harness), and ax-engine-tui. The dependency rule is: core has no web/async/serialization dependencies except types crossing process boundaries; sdk owns serde_json and backend routing; server owns tokio/axum/tonic. unsafe_code is forbid workspace-wide except ax-engine-mlx. Edition 2024, Rust 1.85+. Your task: in ax-engine-mlx, introduce a per-thread MlxClosure compile cache for the dense FFN GeGLU helper that is keyed by both ThreadId and last_dim, so repeated prefills and decodes at the same hidden-dim reuse the compiled closure. Falls back to imperative gelu_approx(gate) * x when try_apply fails. The cache must use OnceLock<Mutex<HashMap<...>>> (process-static, not thread_local!, to avoid SIGSEGV at drop on Metal compiled closures). Write the geglu function as it should appear in crates/ax-engine-mlx/src/model/shared/mlp.rs, including the cache definition, the cache-key construction, the Entry::Vacant insertion, and the imperative fallback. Match existing style: no docstrings, only the single non-obvious WHY comment about SIGSEGV-at-drop. Output the function and its module-level cache definition only.
